steps.title: Identify the infinitive phrase requiring separation
steps.content: After Hot Springs, the phrase to collect in a subterranean basin is an infinitive that elaborates on the role of the porous rocks. We need to determine what punctuation properly connects the noun to this infinitive phrase.

steps.title: Apply the rule for punctuation before an elaborating infinitive
steps.content:
An em dash is used to set off and emphasize an infinitive phrase elaborating on a preceding noun.

The em dash in Springs—to collect provides clear separation that marks the infinitive as a key action or purpose.

A comma can introduce an infinitive but offers less emphasis; a colon is not standard for infinitives of purpose; no punctuation obscures whether to is an infinitive marker or preposition.

C is correct.
